steps.title: Read the values from the 1986 data point on the graph
steps.content: At 1986, the graph shows three data points corresponding to the three land use classes: Class VIII is at approximately 50 hectares, Class VI is at approximately 25 hectares, and Class VII is at approximately 50 hectares. Across all three classes in that year, forest patch sizes ranged from a minimum of about 25 hectares to a maximum of about 50 hectares.

steps.title: Match the range to the correct choice
steps.content:
The sentence reads “In that year, they were between _____ and [choice] hectares.” The graph shows that in 1986, patch sizes ranged from approximately 25 to 50 hectares. Choice D completes this with “and 50 hectares,” giving “between 25 and 50 hectares”—the exact minimum and maximum values observed that year. Choices A, B, and C all propose upper bounds (125, 150, and 250 hectares) that far exceed the highest value actually recorded on the graph.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
